reasoning: Qwen's unsafe findings are false positives. The appcast.example.xml is a test file not used in build/install, and build.sh isn't invoked by PKGBUILD. Deepseek's analysis of build-relevant files shows no actual risks.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen overreacted to inert files (appcast.example.xml) and unused scripts (build.sh), while Deepseek focused on build-critical components.
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to recognize build.sh isn't executed by PKGBUILD
Deepseek missed appcast.example.xml but it's irrelevant
false_positives: appcast.example.xml: Example config not used in build/install
build.sh: Not invoked by PKGBUILD during AUR build process
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Focus on PKGBUILD-invoked scripts
Better context awareness: Distinguish build-time vs source tree files
Reduce false positives on example/test/config files
